Tests the behavior of the `delete` operator on a function in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that defines a function [`x`](<#01-008x>) and then uses an `assert` statement to verify two conditions. The first condition checks that the `delete` operator does not remove the function [`x`](<#01-008x>), which should return `false` because functions declared in this manner are not deletable. The second condition confirms that the type of [`x`](<#01-008x>) is `"function"`. This script demonstrates the behavior of the `delete` operator on function declarations and serves as a test to ensure that the function [`x`](<#01-008x>) remains defined and of the correct type.
